reasoning: France has been installed as outright favorite after a convincing opening win over Senegal and Spain's shock 0-0 draw with Cape Verde, with Les Bleus combining elite depth (Mbappé plus emerging talents Cherki, Barcola and Doué) and a fit Saliba anchoring one of the tournament's strongest defenses. Spain remains the co-second favorite with England despite losing Fermín López to injury and dropping points early, as the Euro 2024 champions retain Yamal, Rodri and Nico Williams. England's win over Croatia and a soft remaining path under Tuchel keep them live, while holders Argentina (Messi hat-trick start) and a deep but inconsistent Brazil missing Rodrygo and Estêvão round out the top tier. Morocco is the dark horse after holding Brazil to a draw and reaching the 2022 semis, but the host advantage and altitude in Mexico, plus a forgiving 48-team bracket, make this an unusually open field — hence low confidence in any single pick this early.
